_comment: Hard thresholds that trigger requires_immediate_attention=True regardless of risk score. When uncertain, add it here — false alarms are acceptable, missed criticals are not.

thresholds.reason: Possible uncontrolled diabetes or hyperglycaemic crisis
Poor long-term glycaemic control — significant complication risk
Markedly elevated liver enzyme — possible acute liver injury
Markedly elevated liver enzyme — possible acute liver injury
Possible acute or severe chronic kidney failure
Severely elevated urea — possible kidney failure or dehydration crisis
Hyperkalaemia — risk of life-threatening cardiac arrhythmia
Severe hypokalaemia — risk of cardiac arrhythmia and muscle paralysis
Severe hypernatraemia — risk of neurological damage
Severe hyponatraemia — risk of cerebral oedema and seizures
Markedly elevated white cell count — possible infection, leukaemia, or inflammatory crisis
Severe leucopenia — high risk of serious infection
Severe hyperbilirubinaemia — possible acute liver failure or biliary obstruction
Severe hypercalcaemia — risk of cardiac and renal complications
Severe hypocalcaemia — risk of tetany and cardiac arrhythmia
